verses.text: The law, 'a shadow of good things to come,' could never with its sacrifices make the comers perfect; 'it is not possible that the blood of bulls... should take away sins.' Shadows. Animal blood could not cleanse the soul.

Christ said, 'Sacrifice... thou wouldest not, but a body hast thou prepared me... Lo, I come... to do thy will, O God.' Willing Saviour. He came to offer Himself in obedience.
Christ said, 'Sacrifice... thou wouldest not, but a body hast thou prepared me... Lo, I come... to do thy will, O God.' Willing Saviour. He came to offer Himself in obedience.
Christ said, 'Sacrifice... thou wouldest not, but a body hast thou prepared me... Lo, I come... to do thy will, O God.' Willing Saviour. He came to offer Himself in obedience.
He takes away the first that He may establish the second; 'we are sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.' Made holy. His one offering sets us apart to God.
He takes away the first that He may establish the second; 'we are sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.' Made holy. His one offering sets us apart to God.
He takes away the first that He may establish the second; 'we are sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.' Made holy. His one offering sets us apart to God.

Every priest stood daily offering; but this man, after one sacrifice, 'sat down'; 'by one offering he hath perfected for ever them that are sanctified.' Finished work. One offering perfects His people forever.

The Holy Ghost witnesses the new covenant: 'their sins... will I remember no more.' Where remission is, 'there is no more offering for sin.' Full pardon. Forgiven sins need no further sacrifice.

Having 'boldness to enter into the holiest by the blood of Jesus,' a new and living way, 'let us draw near... in full assurance of faith.' Bold access. Christ's blood opens the way to God.

Hold fast our profession; 'consider one another to provoke unto love'; 'not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together.' Keep meeting. Believers need one another, the more as the day nears.

If we sin wilfully after knowing the truth, there remains 'a certain fearful looking for of judgment.' To tread Christ's blood underfoot is dreadful. Grave warning. Despising the gospel invites sorer punishment.

'Vengeance belongeth unto me'; 'it is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.' Holy dread. God will judge His own adversaries.
'Vengeance belongeth unto me'; 'it is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.' Holy dread. God will judge His own adversaries.
Recall former days, when you 'endured a great fight of afflictions,' taking joyfully the spoiling of your goods. Past courage. Remembered grace strengthens present faith.

Cast not away your confidence; 'ye have need of patience'; 'the just shall live by faith,' and we are 'of them that believe to the saving of the soul.' Endure. The just live by faith and persevere to life.
